$comment: GOV-1763. Entries here SKIP a specific URL in tools/verify-sources.mjs's source-URL verification. Every entry must name a reason a human can audit — this file is reviewed like a schema PR, not a place to silence a real failure. Prefer fixing the citation (e.g. pointing it at an explicit web.archive.org snapshot) over adding an entry here.

entries.reason: GOV-3159. Independently re-confirmed live via curl (HEAD and GET, both HTTP 200, content-type application/msword, content-length 428032, matching this document's cited sha256) immediately before this entry was added. tools/verify-sources.mjs's own Node fetch() fails on this exact URL with a reproducible `UND_ERR_RES_CONTENT_LENGTH_MISMATCH` (undici's strict response parser rejects this server's response), not a 404/DNS failure — a Node-fetch-vs-server-quirk incompatibility, not evidence of a fabricated or nonexistent source. curl (used throughout this repo's own verification workflow, including by this schema's own VERIFICATION.md) has no such issue with this host.

GOV-3159. Independently re-confirmed live via curl (HTTP 200) immediately before this entry was added. tools/verify-sources.mjs's own Node fetch() fails on this host with a reproducible `HTTPParserError: Invalid header value char` — this server's Content-Security-Policy response header spans multiple raw newlines without RFC 7230 header-folding, which Node's strict undici HTTP parser rejects outright (curl tolerates it without issue). A Node-fetch-vs-server-quirk incompatibility, not evidence of a fabricated or nonexistent source.

GOV-3159. Independently re-confirmed live via curl (HTTP 200, article body names Form ITX201.01.E verbatim) immediately before this entry was added. Same reproducible Node undici `HTTPParserError: Invalid header value char` on this host as the tra.go.tz root entry above (this server's own malformed/unfolded multi-line CSP header, not a bot-block or a missing page).

GOV-3403. Independently re-confirmed live immediately before this entry was added: a plain curl request with no User-Agent override, and tools/verify-sources.mjs's own custom `GovSchema-source-verifier/1` User-Agent, both receive HTTP 403 from this host, while an identical request with a realistic desktop Chrome User-Agent string receives HTTP 200 (confirmed with curl directly, and separately with a full Playwright/Chromium session that rendered the live page and its plate-reservation modal in full). A narrow bot-mitigation rule keyed on User-Agent, not a login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate on the content itself and not evidence of a fabricated or nonexistent source — the same class of false positive already documented above for tra.go.tz, and elsewhere in this registry for Japan's moj.go.jp family (see CATALOG.md's Known Gaps section, GOV-2005).
